select_when: People need fast, reliable navigation through a messy task and cannot recompute the whole workflow from first principles every time.
The environment is repeatable enough for good rules of thumb or interface conventions to reduce friction without pretending to model everything.
A team needs practical triage rules, communication shortcuts, or design conventions that lower cognitive burden quickly.
Starting simple with heuristics would reveal the size and shape of the problem before anyone builds a heavier model.
avoid_when: A once-useful shortcut has hardened into doctrine even though the task, audience, or system has changed.
The problem is genuinely novel and is being forced into a familiar heuristic frame instead of being tested from multiple cuts.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on practical low-friction rules, cognitive-load reduction, and reconnaissance-before-complexity rather than generic heuristic theory.
donor_drops: Did not widen the model into general decision theory because the raw source is stronger on usable shortcuts, simple starts, and anti-hammer warnings.
open_questions: The source is broad enough to touch communication and problem-structuring surfaces, so the typed output is reviewed compression.

select_when.note: The strengths and playbook sections directly support fast navigation, repeatable rules of thumb, practical decision aids, and starting simple.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against shortcuts hardening into doctrine and against applying a familiar frame to a novel problem.

input_type.note: The source activates around friction, overload, and the need for practical shortcuts, while the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields rules of thumb, simple starting structures, and convention-setting guidance; the output label compresses that surface.

reasoning_types.note: The source treats shortcuts as things to inspect and calibrate rather than to trust blindly, so the normalized reasoning labels emphasize diagnosis and reflective checking.
